beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make a pour-over coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Kwame starts his Monday with full energy; he refuses drip coffee so he takes time for a proper pour-over ritual to set a good tone.
Sunny morning, Light Rail closure doesn't affect his route to Kirkland; he drives across town to his workplace.

beats.activity: Work on custom furniture commission and workshop tasks
beats.reasoning: Kwame settles into his morning woodworking routine; he's aware of the USA-Wales match at 11am but isn't particularly invested in soccer, so he works through until lunch time.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a local Kirkland spot, chat with a friend who texts
beats.reasoning: Lunch break during peak sunny weather; the FIFA match airs now but Kwame isn't a huge soccer fan, so he skips it and enjoys the day outdoors instead.
Afternoon commute back toward home; Kwame finishes his work day early on this beautiful Monday to make the most of the weather.

beats.activity: Thrift shopping at local vintage stores
beats.reasoning: Sunny day and free time; Kwame takes advantage to hunt for vintage finds and furniture pieces that might inspire his custom work.

beats.activity: Play chess with a close friend at a local café
beats.reasoning: Kwame prefers time with close friends over crowds; he meets up for an afternoon chess match and conversation in the nice weather.

beats.activity: Walk home through Capitol Hill neighborhood
beats.reasoning: Pleasant afternoon stroll home on a sunny day; Kwame soaks in the weather and enjoys the neighborhood vibe.

beats.activity: Prep dinner and relax at home
beats.reasoning: Kwame cooks a simple dinner and unwinds from his day; laying-back approach means he's content to stay in and recharge.

beats.activity: Watch a documentary and read about woodworking techniques
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down with low-key entertainment; Kwame balances relaxation with his passion for craftsmanship.

diary:
Started the day with my usual ritual—pour-over coffee while the sun hit the kitchen window just right. The drive to Kirkland was smooth, and I spent the morning lost in the furniture commission, which is exactly where I need to be right now. The piece is coming together beautifully. Grabbed lunch at Cafe Juanita and caught up with a friend over text; nice to stay connected even while working.

Afternoon felt a little wandering. Hit up Goodwill on the way back, found some old wood samples that might work for a future project. Then chess with my close friend at Molly Moon's—we played three games, and I finally won one. The walk home through Capitol Hill was perfect weather, clear skies, everything green. Cooked something simple for dinner, then spent the evening reading about joinery techniques. Felt calm, productive without rushing.
thought_process: I deliberately stayed focused on the workshop this morning because that commission has a deadline creeping up, and I wanted to make real progress before my mind scattered. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I'm driving to Kirkland anyway, though I noticed the World Cup buzz downtown—bars were gearing up early. I chose to skip that scene; honestly, I'm not that invested, and the workshop time felt more valuable. By afternoon I was ready for a mental break, so the thrifting and chess were exactly what I needed. The weather was too good to waste indoors, so I walked instead of driving home. Reading about woodworking before bed keeps my hands engaged even when I'm resting.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
